annotations.anchor_quote: I. FACTORS WHICH MAKES WATER An FINITE SOURCE DESPITE ITS APPARENT ABUNDANCE:
annotations.correction: I. FACTORS WHICH MAKE WATER A FINITE SOURCE DESPITE ITS APPARENT ABUNDANCE:
annotations.comment: Revise header for grammar and develop outline with analytical depth, clear stance on proverb, and balanced sections rather than descriptive lists.

annotations.anchor_quote: Countries on equator belt, like Pakistan have lost 1/3rd of their glaciers Since 1960s-2025
annotations.correction: Countries on the equator belt, like Pakistan, have lost one-third of their glaciers since the 1960s-2025
annotations.comment: Add articles, commas, and standardize fractions/dates for clarity and correctness.

annotations.anchor_quote: Countries on equator belt, like Pakistan

annotations.comment: Pakistan is not on the equator belt; replace with accurate examples and verify UN report source.

annotations.anchor_quote: (world Bank report

annotations.comment: Capitalize proper nouns in citations for professionalism.

annotations.anchor_quote: 3. Horrific wastage of water as per statistics CASE IN POINT: In Pakistan 60% of the consumable water goes to waste annually, and agriculture makes 80% of that waste.
annotations.correction: 3. Water wastage due to inefficient practices: CASE IN POINT: In Pakistan, 60% of consumable water is wasted annually, with agriculture accounting for 80%; analyze causes like flood irrigation and effects on scarcity.
annotations.comment: Expand beyond description to include critical analysis of causes, effects, and links to proverb.

annotations.anchor_quote: 5. Lack of water storage reservoirs CASE IN POINT: In Pakistan only 13 major dams and water reservoirs available with 13.7 MAF live Storage only.
annotations.correction: 5. Lack of water storage reservoirs. Case in point: Pakistan has only 13 major dams and reservoirs with 13.7 MAF live storage, leading to significant wastage during monsoons.
annotations.comment: Sub-points are merely descriptive listings with examples; add analytical depth by linking each factor to how it makes water finite despite abundance.

annotations.anchor_quote: FINITE WATER RESOURE

annotations.comment: Spelling error in 'RESOURE'; correct to 'resource' for clarity.

annotations.anchor_quote: In Pakistan only 13 major dams and water reservoirs available with 13.7 MAF live Storage only.

annotations.comment: Factual statistic on dams lacks source; attribute to a reliable source to verify accuracy.

annotations.anchor_quote: CASE INPOINT:
annotations.correction: Case in point:
annotations.comment: Missing space and capitalization error in 'CASE INPOINT'; correct to 'Case in point'.

annotations.anchor_quote: Tragedy of the Common

annotations.anchor_quote: 3. Use of water conservation techniques in agriculture. CASE IN POINT: "Crop per drop" techniques

annotations.anchor_quote: begining at school level.

annotations.anchor_quote: II. DECODING THE PROVERB: DO NOT WASTE WATER EVEN IF YOU ARE STANDING AT A RUNNING STREM .

annotations.anchor_quote: The present condition of scarcity across the hel water globe makes the ages old proverb: do not waste water even if you are Standing at a running stream? much timely and relevant. It helps in individuals' recalling of the reality that water must be conserved in and under all circumstances in order to maintain all life forms on this planet.
annotations.correction: The present condition of scarcity makes the age-old proverb 'Do not waste water even if you are standing at a running stream' timely and relevant, as it underscores conservation despite abundance; this essay argues that factors like climate change and overconsumption render water finite, requiring ethical practices and policies.
annotations.comment: Introduction restates proverb relevance but lacks explicit thesis and roadmap of main arguments; add clear stance on proverb interpretation and outline of key factors and suggestions.

annotations.anchor_quote: ages old proverb
